commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7bdc0b92eb487fde323fe8712ff51d9b9b504b54)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds ClawScan to the ecosystem catalog's Agent Infrastructure & SDKs section with site/repo links, local logo/banner assets, and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a static catalog/content PR rather than a bug report. The relevant checks are link reachability and rendered current-head page proof; the external ClawScan site now returns 200, but rendered PR-card proof is not posted.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed for real rendered proof, and the mechanical changelog cleanup should happen before normal maintainer review proceeds.

target.title: feat(ecosystem): add ClawScan
target.body:
## Summary

- add ClawScan to the ecosystem under Agent Infrastructure & SDKs
- link the public ClawScan site and repository
- add local logo/banner assets, with the banner resized to the catalog's display scale
- credit @Patrick-Erichsen and the original proposal in #177

## Why this replaces #177

The original contribution is sound, but GitHub reports that maintainer edits are disabled. This version preserves the contributor's content and co-author credit while placing the developer/security CLI in the current infrastructure taxonomy and adding the repository changelog entry.

## Verification

- `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
- `bun test` — 23 passed
- `bun run test:built-assets`
- production preview `/ecosystem` returned 200
- built ClawScan PNG and SVG returned 200 with the expected media types
- built HTML contains the ClawScan card, description, site/repository links, logo, banner, and Agent Infrastructure & SDKs section
- `https://clawscanner.app` resolves on IPv4/IPv6 and returns HTTP 200 with the canonical ClawScan content

## Review

- Auto Review: one link-reachability finding rejected after fresh DNS and HTTP 200 proof; no accepted/actionable findings remain
- public model identifier gate: not applicable; this static catalog change introduces no model identifiers
target.author: steipete
